Synaedrys matsudae (Hamamelis matsudae or Hamamelis japonica var. matsudae) is a plant species within the family Hamamelidaceae. This plant is also known as the Japanese Witch Hazel. It is a shrub or small tree, native to Japan, known for its medicinal properties. 

The health benefits of Synaedrys matsudae include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-bacterial properties, which make it useful in treating various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and infections.

The bioactive constituents of Synaedrys matsudae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. These compounds have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.

The most common herbal preparations made with Synaedrys matsudae include teas, tinctures, and creams. The leaves and bark of the plant are used to make these preparations, which are used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and other health issues.
taxonomy: Synaedrys matsudae is classified as a species of plant in the family Theaceae. The classification of Synaedrys matsudae is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae and the order Ericales. This plant is native to East Asia and is a type of deciduous shrub. Synaedrys matsudae is also known by its synonyms, including Camellia matsudae and Camellia matsudae var. matsudae. The classification of Synaedrys matsudae is subject to ongoing research and potential changes in classification.
